summary: Verified strip-emergent.js run against a real CRA production build. CRITICAL BUG: the stripped build/index.html is broken and the Netlify deploy will render a blank white page. All 4 Emergent artifacts are indeed removed, but the posthog <script> regex is over-greedy on minified HTML and also swallows the CRA main.js <script> tag, the main CSS <link>, the <body> opening tag, and <div id="root">. Dev-mode public/index.html is untouched (md5 unchanged) so the Emergent editor path is preserved as intended.

ui_bugs.issue: Regex /<script\b[^>]*>[\s\S]*?posthog\.init[\s\S]*?<\/script>/gi anchors on the FIRST <script> tag in the document (the DataCloneError shim in <head>) and its lazy [\s\S]*? expands across the entire page — swallowing the CRA main script <script defer src="/static/js/main.f5a34292.js"></script>, and everything else between the head and the posthog closing tag. Stripped build/index.html is 1315 bytes and contains: NO <div id="root">, NO CRA main.js <script>, NO main.css <link>, NO <body> opening tag, NO <noscript>. Result: React never mounts, page is blank white. Verified via `npx serve -s build`: GET / returns 200 with a 1315-byte HTML body, Playwright shows document.getElementById('root') === null, document.scripts.length === 0, wordmark never appears, screenshot is fully white.

integration_issues.issue: netlify.toml build command `yarn install && yarn build && node scripts/strip-emergent.js` will publish a broken build/ directory. Every route (/, /work, /about, /writing, /connect, /work/manufacturing-performance, /writing/inside-out) will serve the same 1315-byte gutted HTML and render a blank page in the browser.

action_items: Fix strip-emergent.js: the posthog and DataCloneError regexes must NOT anchor on any earlier <script> tag. Recommended fix: match the script by its content signature scoped to its own tag, e.g. /<script\b[^>]*>(?:(?!<\/script>)[\s\S])*?posthog\.init(?:(?!<\/script>)[\s\S])*?<\/script>\s*/gi (tempered greedy token so the match cannot cross a </script> boundary). Apply the same tempered pattern to the DataCloneError regex.

After fixing, re-run: `cd /app/frontend && rm -rf build && CI=false DISABLE_ESLINT_PLUGIN=true GENERATE_SOURCEMAP=false yarn build && node scripts/strip-emergent.js` and confirm build/index.html STILL contains:
,

Consider adding a post-strip sanity check inside strip-emergent.js that fails (process.exit(1)) if the output is missing `id="root"` or the CRA main.*.js script tag — that would have caught this before any deploy.
critical_code_review_comments: strip-emergent.js: lazy [\s\S]*? across a minified HTML with multiple <script> tags is unsafe — a lazy quantifier does not stop at intermediate </script> tags because [\s\S] matches everything including </script>. Use a tempered greedy token (?:(?!<\/script>)[\s\S])*? to constrain to a single script element.

The removals[] log is misleading: it reported only `posthog analytics init` (3 items), but the DataCloneError shim was actually also removed — because the posthog regex ate it (and much more) as collateral. The script has no way to know it destroyed the CRA app. Add an assertion step.

netlify.toml build command has no verification step — a strip failure produces a green build but a broken deploy. Recommend chaining `&& grep -q 'id="root"' build/index.html && grep -q 'static/js/main' build/index.html` after the strip.

success_rate.frontend: 20% (only 1 of 5 acceptance checks pass: source public/index.html untouched; the stripped-build-still-renders check FAILS)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Reproduce with: `cd /app/frontend && rm -rf build && CI=false DISABLE_ESLINT_PLUGIN=true GENERATE_SOURCEMAP=false yarn build && node scripts/strip-emergent.js && wc -c build/index.html && grep -c 'id="root"' build/index.html`. Expected after fix: build/index.html ~4-5KB, contains id="root" and static/js/main.*.js, and grep for emergent-badge/emergent-main.js/posthog/DataCloneError returns 0. Then serve locally with `npx serve -s build -l 4321` and confirm Playwright sees nav-wordmark / home-hero-wordmark, window.posthog undefined, and #emergent-badge count = 0. Dev URL https://method-positioning.preview.emergentagent.com must continue to show all 4 Emergent artifacts (it does today: md5 of public/index.html is 0c8747fed0b2131093d543d91784e785, 9072 bytes, unchanged).

rca of the issue.symptom: Post-strip build/index.html is 1315 bytes; contains no <div id="root">, no CRA main script tag, no main CSS link, no <body> opening tag. Served via `npx serve` returns HTTP 200 with a body-less HTML shell; browser renders a blank white page and React never mounts.
rca of the issue.root_cause:
In strip-emergent.js, the posthog removal regex `/]*>[\s\S]*?posthog\.init[\s\S]*?<\/script>\s*/gi` uses a lazy [\s\S]*? that is NOT tempered against . In the minified CRA build, the FIRST , the boundary, the
, the entire … block, and the opening ``. Net effect: one regex `.replace()` deletes ~4342 bytes of essential markup along with the intended 3 targets.
reproduction_steps: npx serve -s build -l 4321 & sleep 2 && curl -s http://localhost:4321/ | head -c 400   # returns gutted HTML
rca of the issue.mitigation: Use a tempered greedy quantifier that cannot cross a </script> boundary. Replace both the posthog and DataCloneError regexes with, e.g.: `/<script\b[^>]*>(?:(?!<\/script>)[\s\S])*?posthog\.init(?:(?!<\/script>)[\s\S])*?<\/script>\s*/gi` and `/<script\b[^>]*>(?:(?!<\/script>)[\s\S])*?DataCloneError(?:(?!<\/script>)[\s\S])*?<\/script>\s*/gi`. Also add a post-strip guard: `if (!/id="root"/.test(html) || !/static\/js\/main\.[^"]+\.js/.test(html)) { console.error('[strip-emergent] Sanity check failed'); process.exit(1); }` so a bad strip fails the build instead of publishing a broken deploy.

verified_ok: /app/frontend/public/index.html is byte-untouched (md5 0c8747fed0b2131093d543d91784e785, 9072 bytes). Dev preview URL https://method-positioning.preview.emergentagent.com/ still serves emergent-badge (2 matches), emergent-main.js (1), posthog (9), DataCloneError (1) — Emergent editor path preserved as intended.
/app/netlify.toml build command ends with `&& node scripts/strip-emergent.js`.
yarn build completes with exit code 0 (Compiled successfully, 133.32 kB gzipped main JS).
Strip removes all 4 artifacts from build/index.html (grep count 0 for each after strip).
But: the strip ALSO removes essential CRA markup — see rca.
